Title: Occurrence and biosynthesis of plant sesterterpenes (C25), a new addition to terpene diversity

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with allylic prenyl diphosphate substrates to generate geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP, C25); biochemical assignment in Arabidopsis was reported as differing between studies, with AtGGPPS1 generally behaving as a GGPPS unless key floor-2 residue is mutated.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GFPP (in some assays/mutants); GGPP as maintained activity in Wang et al. 2016 context
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized metabolites with reported ecological and pharmacological activities depending on structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides or can be converted toward GFPP-producing activity relevant to sesterterpene precursor formation, but physiological role remains unclear and native enzyme function is debated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; structural comparison; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGGPPS1/AtGFPPS1 was discussed as a GGPPS-like enzyme relevant to chain-length determination in Arabidopsis. 2. Biochemical results differed between studies, possibly due to recombinant tag effects. 3. Mutation of Arg132 to smaller residues conferred clear GFPPS activity, supporting residue-based control of GFPP production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review article. Native AtGGPPS1 biochemical assignment is conflicting across cited studies; included because it is summarized as part of core GFPPS evolution/mechanism.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with allylic prenyl diphosphates to produce geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP, C25) as the major product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sesterterpene biosynthesis by generating the C25 precursor GFPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; crystal structure comparison
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGFPPS2 was one of the first plant GFPPSs identified. 2. It produces GFPP as the main product in vitro from IPP plus allylic prenyl diphosphates. 3. Structural comparison helped define residue determinants distinguishing GFPPS from GGPPS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review synthesis based on Nagel et al. 2015 and Wang et al. 2016.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with allylic prenyl diphosphate substrates to form GFPP, the C25 precursor of sesterterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Arabidopsis root sesterterpene biosynthesis by supplying GFPP precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; tissue expression analysis; GFPP detection in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGFPPS3 produces GFPP in vitro. 2. GFPPS activity and GFPP were detected in Arabidopsis roots where AtGFPPS3 is predominantly expressed. 3. It contributes to precursor supply for root sesterterpene formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as physiologically relevant root-expressed GFPPS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with allylic prenyl diphosphate substrates to produce GFPP, the precursor of sesterterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Arabidopsis root sesterterpene biosynthesis by generating GFPP precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in vitro enzyme assay; tissue expression analysis; GFPP detection in roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGFPPS4 was identified as a plant GFPPS producing GFPP in vitro. 2. It is predominantly expressed in Arabidopsis roots. 3. Together with AtGFPPS3 it is linked to endogenous root GFPP and sesterterpene biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP), the C25 precursor for leucosceptroid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: IPP with allylic prenyl diphosphate acceptor(s)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MEP inhibitor fosmidomycin decreases leucosceptroid B; MVA inhibitor mevinolin increases leucosceptroid B
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of leucosceptroid-type sesterterpenes by providing GFPP precursor in glandular trichomes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene isolation; in vitro functional identification; inhibitor treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcaGFPPS1 was isolated from Leucosceptrum canum glandular trichomes accumulating leucosceptroids. 2. It was functionally identified as a GFPPS for C25 precursor biosynthesis. 3. Inhibitor experiments supported that leucosceptroid biosynthesis derives from the plastidial MEP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogeny suggests independent evolution of Lamiaceae GFPPS from Brassicaceae GFPPS.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP) into the sesterterpene skeleton (+)-thalianatriene (C6).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces the Arabidopsis sesterterpene skeleton (+)-thalianatriene from GFPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; product purification and structural elucidation; crystal structure; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS18 was among the first characterized plant sesterTPSs. 2. It cyclizes GFPP to (+)-thalianatriene via a type-A cyclization route. 3. Crystal structure and mutagenesis identified catalytic pocket residues controlling sesterTPS activity and product specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Asn493, Gly328, Tyr462/Phe, Tyr465, and Phe496/Tyr were highlighted as important residues; F496C mutant altered product outcome.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to generate the sesterterpene skeleton (-)-retigeranin B (C7).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces the Arabidopsis sesterterpene skeleton (-)-retigeranin B from GFPP.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; product purification and structural elucidation; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS19 was one of the first plant sesterTPSs with product structurally identified. 2. It uses GFPP to form (-)-retigeranin B. 3. Mutagenesis at catalytic-pocket glycine abolished sesterTPS activity and enabled use of shorter prenyl diphosphates.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms root-associated sesterterpene C8 that significantly affects root microbiota assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway reconstruction in E. coli or Nicotiana benthamiana; structural elucidation; in planta detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS25 produces (-)-ent-quiannulatene from GFPP. 2. C8 was detected in Arabidopsis roots. 3. Together with C9, this sesterterpene significantly influences root microbiota assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: G328W mutation abolished sesterTPS activity and enabled utilization of shorter prenyl diphosphate substrates.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms root-associated sesterterpene C9 that significantly affects root microbiota assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous pathway reconstruction in E. coli or Nicotiana benthamiana; structural elucidation; in planta detection
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS30 produces (+)-astellatene from GFPP. 2. C9 was detected in Arabidopsis roots. 3. Together with C8, this compound significantly influences root microbiota assembly in a similar pattern.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to produce (-)-arathanadiene A and related product(s).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces the Arabidopsis sesterterpene skeleton (-)-arathanadiene A.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS17 was characterized as a Brassicaceae sesterTPS. 2. It forms (-)-arathanadiene A from GFPP. 3. It contributes to Arabidopsis sesterterpene structural diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Summarized from cited work in the review.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to produce (2E)-flocerene via a type-B cyclization mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly produces the sesterterpene (2E)-flocerene through a distinct type-B cyclization route.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous expression; product identification; mechanistic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS06 is the only plant sesterTPS summarized to use the type-B cyclization pathway. 2. It produces (2E)-flocerene from GFPP. 3. Its catalytic determinants differ from type-A sesterTPSs, underscoring mechanistic complexity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Type-A diagnostic residues cannot be directly applied to AtTPS06.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to produce Capsella rubella sesterterpene skeletons including caprutriene/caprudiene-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces Capsella-specific sesterterpene skeletons from GFPP.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cr237 was identified as a Brassicaceae sesterTPS. 2. It contributes to production of Capsella sesterterpene skeletons. 3. Its characterization broadened the taxonomic distribution of plant sesterTPSs within Brassicaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not provide accession or precise one-product mapping.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to produce Capsella rubella sesterterpene skeletons including caprutriene/caprudiene-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces Capsella-specific sesterterpene skeletons from GFPP.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cr089 was identified as a Brassicaceae sesterTPS. 2. It contributes to production of Capsella sesterterpene skeletons. 3. Its characterization broadened the known sesterTPS repertoire in Brassicaceae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review does not provide accession or precise one-product mapping.
